message:
feat: compact per-message extra payloads for large sessions (>=32 MiB)

Large Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini session files can carry enormous
duplicated JSON in every message's `extra` field. When a session file
exceeds 32 MiB, each connector now replaces the full extra payload with
a compact "cass" envelope that retains only the metadata downstream
consumers need:

- model name
- token usage (input, output, cache read/creation, service tier)
- tool call count (Claude Code)
- attachment references

The token_extraction module is updated to read from the new compact
`/cass/*` paths first, falling back to the original deep paths for
backward compatibility with non-compacted sessions. Key field renames
are also handled (cache_read_tokens vs cache_read_input_tokens, etc.).

Gemini connector additionally switches from read_to_string + from_str
to File::open + BufReader + from_reader, and takes ownership of the
messages array via `remove("messages")` instead of cloning, reducing
peak memory for large files.

Each connector and token_extraction gain corresponding unit tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
